output:
CVE-2021-22166 is a vulnerability in GitLab 13.7 and later versions, which could allow an attacker to launch a denial of service (DoS) attack against Prometheus. This exploit is made possible by sending an HTTP request with a malformed method, thereby consuming resources and potentially causing service disruption.

The vulnerability is classified under CWE-400, indicating a failure to properly control resource consumption, leading to resource exhaustion. Its severity has been rated as HIGH, underscoring the potential impact on affected systems.

The vulnerability affects both the Community and Enterprise editions of GitLab, specifically versions 13.7.0 up to but not including 13.7.2. Organizations should be aware of the associated MITRE ATT&CK technique T1499 as it relates to this type of network flooding.

To mitigate this issue, it is crucial to upgrade to a non-vulnerable version of GitLab. Implementing general security best practices and monitoring for abnormal requests might also help in reducing the attack surface.
